result:
## Shared state between the bio.c background threads and the main thread

**`bioProcessBackgroundJobs`** runs as `BIO_WORKER_NUM` (3) threads, spawned in `bioInit` (bio.c:172). Here's every piece of state it touches that the main thread also touches:

| Shared state | Bg thread access | Main thread access | Synchronization |
|---|---|---|---|
| `bio_jobs[worker]` (per-worker job list) | pops via `listFirst`/`listDelNode` (bio.c:290,365) | pushes via `listAddNodeTail` in `bioSubmitJob` (bio.c:185); length-checked in `bioDrainWorker` (bio.c:387) | `bio_mutex[worker]` (held around every access) |
| `bio_jobs_counter[type]` | decremented after finishing a job (bio.c:366) | incremented in `bioSubmitJob` (bio.c:186); read in `bioPendingJobsOfType` (bio.c:376), called from `server.c`/`aof.c`/`evict.c` | same `bio_mutex[worker]` (the worker owning that job type) |
| `bio_newjob_cond[worker]` | waited on (bio.c:286), also signaled by itself after each job (bio.c:367) | signaled in `bioSubmitJob` (bio.c:187); waited on in `bioDrainWorker` (bio.c:388) | paired with `bio_mutex[worker]` (condvar semantics) |
| `bio_comp_list` (completion-job response list) | appended to via `listAddNodeTail` (bio.c:351) | swapped out (`bio_comp_list = listCreate()`) in `bioPipeReadJobCompList` (bio.c:429-430) | `bio_mutex_comp` |
| `job_comp_pipe[2]` | writes 1 byte to `job_comp_pipe[1]` (bio.c:354) | reads `job_comp_pipe[0]` via the event loop (bio.c:156, 424) | none needed — disjoint ends of the fd, and pipe read/write syscalls are kernel-serialized; the fds themselves are written once in `bioInit` before any thread starts |
| `server.aof_bio_fsync_status` | `atomicSet`/`atomicGet` (bio.c:320-328) | `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` in aof.c, server.c (multiple sites) | C11 `_Atomic` / `__atomic` / `__sync` builtins, relaxed ordering (`atomicvar.h`) — no mutex |
| `server.aof_bio_fsync_errno` | `atomicSet` (bio.c:322) | `atomicGet` in server.c:5220 | same atomic macros |
| `server.fsynced_reploff_pending` | `atomicSet` (bio.c:329) | `atomicSet`/`atomicGet` throughout aof.c, server.c | same atomic macros |
| `server.bio_cpulist` | read once via `redisSetCpuAffinity` (bio.c:267) | set once at config load (`IMMUTABLE_CONFIG`, config.c:3230), before `bioInit` runs | none — write happens-before thread creation, never mutated again |
| `bio_threads[worker]` | not read by the bg thread itself | written in `bioInit` (bio.c:177); read in `bioKillThreads` (debug.c:2456 → bio.c:402-403) on crash, possibly from whatever thread is crashing | none — set once before jobs flow, main-thread-only read path in normal operation |

Read-only/never-mutated after compile time, so not really a race concern: `bio_worker_title[]`, `bio_job_to_worker[]`.

**Notes:**
- All producer-side calls (`bioSubmitJob`/`bioCreateCloseJob`/`bioCreateFsyncJob`/`bioCreateCloseAofJob`/`bioCreateLazyFreeJob`/`bioCreateCompRq`) originate from the main thread (call sites in `lazyfree.c`, `replication.c`, `rdb.c`, `aof.c`, `db.c`, `cluster_asm.c`).
- The three `aof_bio_fsync_*`/`fsynced_reploff_pending` fields are declared `redisAtomic` in `server.h:2309-2376` specifically because they're the cross-thread channel between the AOF-fsync bio worker and the main thread — mutex-free by design, unlike the job-queue state which uses per-worker mutexes + condvars.
- `bio_comp_list` is intentionally decoupled from the per-worker job-queue locks — it has its own dedicated `bio_mutex_comp`, since all three workers' completion jobs funnel into one shared response list.
